change_log_latest:
• 🛠️ Consolidated the retained v0.7 release history into a single `v0.7.0` section and removed all `v0.6.x` entries from `CHANGE_LOG_RELEASE`, shrinking installer metadata without losing the current major-series summary.
• ⚡ Packed `code_syntax.json` into the shipped control payload as gzip+base64, now unpacks it to `/opt/club3090-control/code_syntax.json` on startup, and serves it from `/admin/code-syntax` so the large syntax-config blob no longer rides inside shipped JavaScript.
• 🧰 Audited `control/chat.py`, removed an unused in-memory conversation index path, and kept the chat storage/runtime behavior unchanged while trimming a little more dead weight.
change_log_release:
v0.7.0

• 🛠️ Removed the remaining inert legacy-dual backend stubs, legacy status payload field, dead log-branch special cases, and other no-op compatibility leftovers that no longer participated in live runtime behavior.
• ⚡ Deleted several unreferenced UI runtime formatter functions and collapsed more dead legacy branches so the shipped JavaScript and final installer payload shrink further without changing current scope behavior.
• 📝 Updated the design docs again to reflect the cleaner post-cleanup state and the narrower set of legacy-oriented naming that still remains for non-dual reasons.
• 📝 Rewrote the main design docs to describe the current split-source architecture, canonical scope model, persistence layout, and build pipeline more accurately.
• 📝 Added a measured explanation for why the installer artifact did not shrink after the legacy dual-runtime refactor, including concrete byte deltas and the remaining compatibility residue.
• 📝 Reworked the v0.8 migration model and plan around actual readiness: schema-first normalization, upstream pull/generate wrappers, generated compose handling, and a separate advanced path for imported/custom models.
• 🐞 Fixed the updater readiness probe so successful self-updates no longer log a false admin-listener timeout before completion.
• 🖥️ Reworked the Instances scope strip so Global comes first, GPU tabs stay grouped, built-in and custom pair tabs follow together, and Create Custom Pair now lives on the same row with proper separators.
• 🖥️ Replaced the remaining outlined modal close buttons with the larger plain close style, added a dedicated GPU picker for custom pairs, and upgraded Wake-on-LAN into a validated single-line modal that preloads the configured default MAC.
• ⚡ Stopped syntax highlighting from re-touching live streaming transcript messages, which removes the code-block flicker during generation and preserves highlighting once the reply finishes.
• 🐞 Removed the leftover two-GPU Global summary special case and fixed stray edit/delete icon encoding regressions found during the post-refactor audit.
• ⚙️ Reordered the default dual-selector preference so automatic pair rows now favor the live-validated vllm/dual-dflash recipe before falling back to heavier Qwen dual compose variants.
• 🧪 Confirmed on the live host that the built-in PAIR0_1 can boot on vllm/dual-dflash, answer /v1/models, and then hand the machine back to the original single-GPU runtime state afterward.
• 🐞 Fixed dual-instance compose launches so instance project directories now anchor on the compose file's own folder, which restores relative dual-patch mounts like ../../patches/vllm-marlin-pad/... and prevents PAIR0_1 launches from dropping the required /vllm/ path segment.
• 🧪 Re-ran the live pair-runtime exercise after the stop-path fix and used the failure details to confirm and correct the compose-directory resolution bug before cutting the final candidate.
• 🐞 Fixed stop_instance() so dual and single overlap handoffs no longer run the launch-time GPU memory guard during docker compose down, which allows busy runtimes to stop cleanly before a replacement scope starts.
• 🧪 Revalidated the live controller updater after the v0.7.1 fixes, confirming that staged-feed self-updates now refresh /opt/club3090-control/install-club3090-server.sh to the downloaded version and reset the updater service back to the canonical GitHub source afterward.
• 🐞 Fixed controller self-updates so the updater now downloads the remote installer into a temporary file, refreshes /opt/club3090-control/install-club3090-server.sh, and then executes that cached copy, which keeps the local installer cache aligned with streamed update runs.
• 🔄 Hardened the installer's self-update source normalization so the repo URL, ref, branch, and raw script template always snap back to the canonical GitHub feed instead of persisting temporary override values from a previous update environment.
• 🧪 Verified the corrected updater path against the live server by driving the real controller update flow from a staged test feed, waiting through service recovery, and confirming the updated runtime/status surface came back healthy on the host.
• 🧰 Split the backend monolith into the new control/ source tree, renamed the old telemetry slice to system.py, rebuilt the canonical target model around GLOBAL, GPU<n>, and PAIRx_y, removed the legacy dual-runtime behavior path, and made sequential auto-pairs the built-in dual layout while preserving custom pair groups.
• 🖥️ Split the admin frontend into the new web/ source tree, removed the GPU pairing toggle, updated scope and pair handling around automatic sequential pairs, and blocked deletion of built-in auto-pairs while leaving custom pair management intact.
• 🛠️ Reworked the build pipeline around build/, web/, and control/, extracted the smoke harness into build/smoke_tests.py, added the root build.py wrapper, moved build logs into logs/, and regenerated the root monolith artifacts from the split sources.
• 🧪 Refreshed the status smoke fixtures to the canonical auto-pair model and reran the full local build, syntax, minification, UI, HTML, API, chat, and log smoke suite successfully against the new layout.
• 📝 Added v07_PROGRESS_REPORT.MD and v07_CHECKLIST.MD so the v0.7 refactor track can be resumed cleanly mid-stream if the work is ever interrupted.
